data.narrative:
Chariot Defense is a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $41M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $41M total raised as a baseline proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $82M. Peer-median analysis uses three same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $300M and a range of $150M to $500M. This reflects the valuation bandwidth observed across Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $70M to $345M, with a median of $191M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty in how Chariot Defense maps onto observed peer multiples—specifically, whether the company's metrics and market position align with the lower quartile (Ares Industries) or upper quartile (Stark Defence) of the peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and contract backlog visibility relative to comparables. Additional operational and technical diligence would be required to narrow this range materially.
